title_en: Syrups of Currants, Bitter Oranges, Cherries, and Raspberries
title_it: Di ribes, di amarene, di ciliege e di lamponi
section_en: Preparation of Syrups from Acidic Fruits

ingredients.name_en: preserved fruit juice (currant, bitter orange, cherry, or raspberry)

steps_en: Pour decanted and filtered preserved juice into refined sugar in the given proportion.
Heat rapidly while stirring with a spatula.
Once boiling ceases, remove from heat.
Let rest briefly, remove the foam formed.
notes_en: Applies commonly to currant, bitter orange, cherry, and raspberry syrups made from their respective preserved juices.

section_en: Preparation of Syrups from Acidic Fruits
steps_en: Heat ground cochineal with water to boiling, adding tartaric acid.
Prepare a base syrup from refined sugar and water according to usual rules, clarified.
Pour the cochineal solution into the base syrup.
Add a solution of citric acid and tartaric acid dissolved in water.
Strain through wool using paper pulp.
